annotations.label: Hopf algebra (informal)

anchor.snippet: is a structure that is simultaneously a

annotations.note: `HopfAlgebra R A` is defined as a bialgebra equipped with an antipode satisfying the antipode axioms.

annotations.label: Convolution algebra of a bialgebra

anchor.snippet: One can consider the convolution algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the convolution (semi)ring structure on `WithConv (C →ₗ[R] A)` for any coalgebra `C` and algebra `A`, specializing to the convolution algebra of a bialgebra.

annotations.label: Hopf algebra (antipode condition)

anchor.snippet: is said to be a Hopf algebra if the identity of

annotations.note: The `mul_antipode_rTensor_comul` and `mul_antipode_lTensor_comul` fields of `HopfAlgebra` encode exactly the antipode axioms `μ ∘ (S ⊗ id) ∘ Δ = η ∘ ε = μ ∘ (id ⊗ S) ∘ Δ`.

annotations.label: Hopf algebra over a commutative ring

anchor.snippet: one can replace the underlying field K with a commutative ring R

annotations.note: Mathlib's `HopfAlgebra R A` is already defined over an arbitrary `CommSemiring R`, which covers (and generalizes) the commutative ring case.

annotations.label: Self-duality of Hopf algebra definition

anchor.snippet: The definition of Hopf algebra is self-dual

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the self-duality of the Hopf-algebra axioms as a formal proposition.

annotations.label: Structure constants for multiplication, comultiplication, and antipode

anchor.snippet: one may define the algebra in terms of structure constants for multiplication

annotations.note: No development of Hopf-algebra structure constants relative to a fixed basis is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associativity constraint on structure constants

anchor.snippet: Associativity then requires that

annotations.note: No structure-constant formulation of associativity is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Co-associativity constraint on structure constants

anchor.snippet: while co-associativity requires that

annotations.note: No structure-constant formulation of coassociativity is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Connecting axiom on structure constants

anchor.snippet: The connecting axiom requires that

annotations.note: No structure-constant formulation of the bialgebra compatibility axiom is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invertibility of the antipode in special cases

anchor.snippet: The antipode S is sometimes required to have a K -linear inverse

annotations.note: Only the special case `S(S(a)) = a` for group-like elements is proved; bijectivity of `S` on commutative or finite-dimensional Hopf algebras is listed as TODO in `HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Antipode is an antihomomorphism

anchor.snippet: In general, S is an antihomomorphism

annotations.note: `HopfAlgebra.antipode_mul` proves `antipode R (a * b) = antipode R b * antipode R a`.

annotations.label: Involutive Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: then the Hopf algebra is said to be involutive

annotations.note: There is no Mathlib class for Hopf algebras with `S² = id`.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of antipode

anchor.snippet: If a bialgebra B admits an antipode S , then S is unique

annotations.note: The `HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean` TODO explicitly lists uniqueness of the Hopf algebra structure on a bialgebra as not yet done.

annotations.label: Antipode as group-inverse analog

anchor.snippet: The antipode is an analog to the inversion map on a group

annotations.note: Mathlib realizes the analogy on group-like elements: `GroupLike.toUnits` turns group-likes into units with inverse given by the antipode, and `IsGroupLikeElem.antipode_mul_cancel` / `mul_antipode_cancel` are the unit and inverse laws.

annotations.label: Hopf subalgebra

anchor.snippet: A subalgebra A of a Hopf algebra H is a Hopf subalgebra if it is a subcoalgebra

annotations.note: No `HopfSubalgebra` / sub-Hopf-algebra structure is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nichols–Zoeller freeness theorem

anchor.snippet: The Nichols–Zoeller freeness theorem of Warren Nichols and Bettina Zoeller

annotations.note: The Nichols–Zoeller freeness theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf subalgebra of semisimple is semisimple

anchor.snippet: a Hopf subalgebra of a semisimple finite-dimensional Hopf algebra is automatically semisimple

annotations.note: This consequence of Nichols–Zoeller is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right normal Hopf subalgebra

anchor.snippet: is said to be right normal in a Hopf algebra H if it satisfies the condition of stability

annotations.note: Right adjoint actions and normality of Hopf subalgebras are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left normal Hopf subalgebra

anchor.snippet: a Hopf subalgebra A is left normal in H if it is stable under the left adjoint mapping

annotations.note: Left adjoint actions and normality of Hopf subalgebras are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Normal Hopf subalgebra

anchor.snippet: The two conditions of normality are equivalent if the antipode S is bijective

annotations.note: Normality of Hopf subalgebras is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Normal Hopf subalgebra condition and quotient

anchor.snippet: A normal Hopf subalgebra A in H satisfies the condition

annotations.note: The Hopf quotient `H/HA⁺` and the corresponding condition on normal subalgebras are not formalized.

annotations.label: Hopf order

anchor.snippet: A Hopf order O over an integral domain R with field of fractions K is an order

annotations.note: Hopf orders are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group-like element

anchor.snippet: A group-like element is a nonzero element x

annotations.note: `IsGroupLikeElem R a` is defined by `ε a = 1` and `Δ a = a ⊗ₜ a`; nonzero-ness follows automatically over a nontrivial ring via `IsGroupLikeElem.ne_zero`.

annotations.label: Group-like elements form a group

anchor.snippet: The group-like elements form a group with inverse given by the antipode

annotations.note: Mathlib equips `GroupLike R A` with a `Group` instance whose inverse is the antipode (`GroupLike.val_inv`).

annotations.label: Primitive element

anchor.snippet: A primitive element x satisfies

annotations.note: No predicate for primitive elements (`Δ x = x ⊗ 1 + 1 ⊗ x`) exists in Mathlib's coalgebra/bialgebra files.

annotations.label: Comultiplication formula on h_k

anchor.snippet: Note that functions on a finite group can be identified with the group ring

annotations.note: The dual Hopf algebra `(R[G])*` of functions on a finite group, together with the indicator-basis comultiplication, is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cohomology algebra of a Lie group as Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: The cohomology algebra (over a field

annotations.note: The Hopf algebra structure on the cohomology ring of a Lie group is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf's structure theorem for cohomology of Lie groups

anchor.snippet: Theorem (Hopf)

annotations.note: Hopf's structure theorem for the cohomology of compact connected Lie groups is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quantum groups as deformed Hopf algebras

anchor.snippet: Other interesting Hopf algebras are certain

annotations.note: Quantum groups such as `U_q(g)` are not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tensor product of A-modules

anchor.snippet: Then, M ⊗ N is also an A -module

annotations.note: Mathlib has a `HopfAlgebra` instance on tensor products of Hopf algebras but not the module-level tensor product of representations using the comultiplication.

annotations.label: Trivial representation

anchor.snippet: we can define the trivial representation as the base field K

annotations.note: The base ring as the trivial Hopf-algebra representation via the counit is not packaged in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual representation

anchor.snippet: the dual representation of A can be defined

annotations.note: The dual representation of a Hopf algebra (via the antipode and transpose) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Natural isomorphisms are A-module homomorphisms

anchor.snippet: the natural isomorphisms of vector spaces M → M ⊗ K and M → K ⊗ M are also isomorphisms of A -modules

annotations.note: The unitor isomorphisms in the monoidal category of Hopf-algebra modules are not formalized.

annotations.label: Graded Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: Graded Hopf algebras are often used in algebraic topology

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define graded Hopf algebras.

annotations.label: Locally compact quantum group

anchor.snippet: Locally compact quantum groups generalize Hopf algebras and carry a topology

annotations.note: Locally compact quantum groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quasi-Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: Quasi-Hopf algebras are generalizations of Hopf algebras

annotations.note: Quasi-Hopf algebras are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplier Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: Multiplier Hopf algebras introduced by Alfons Van Daele in 1994

annotations.note: Multiplier Hopf algebras are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf group-(co)algebra

anchor.snippet: Hopf group-(co)algebras introduced by V. G. Turaev in 2000

annotations.note: Turaev's Hopf group-(co)algebras are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: Weak Hopf algebras , or quantum groupoids, are generalizations of Hopf algebras

annotations.note: Weak Hopf algebras / quantum groupoids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite groupoid algebra as weak Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: a finite groupoid algebra is a weak Hopf algebra

annotations.note: The weak Hopf algebra structure on a finite groupoid algebra is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group axiomatization via Hopf algebra diagrams

anchor.snippet: Groups can be axiomatized by the same diagrams

annotations.note: The equivalence between groups and Hopf monoids in cartesian monoidal categories is listed as a TODO in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Hopf_.lean`.

annotations.label: Hopf algebra in a braided monoidal category

anchor.section: Hopf algebras in braided monoidal categories
anchor.snippet: The definition of Hopf algebra is naturally extended to arbitrary braided monoidal categories

annotations.note: `HopfObj X` is defined for an object `X` in a braided monoidal category as a `BimonObj` with an antipode satisfying `antipode_left` and `antipode_right`.

annotations.label: Bialgebra in a category

anchor.section: Hopf algebras in braided monoidal categories
anchor.snippet: with the properties 1),2),3) is called a bialgebra in the category

annotations.note: `BimonObj M` in a braided monoidal category combines `MonObj M` and `ComonObj M` together with the compatibility axioms, formalizing a bialgebra object.

annotations.label: Groups as Hopf algebras in Set

anchor.section: Hopf algebras in braided monoidal categories
anchor.snippet: In the monoidal category

annotations.note: The identification of `HopfObj` in `Set` (or any cartesian monoidal category) with groups is explicitly listed as TODO in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Hopf_.lean`.

annotations.label: Classical Hopf algebras as Hopf algebras in Vect

anchor.section: Hopf algebras in braided monoidal categories
anchor.snippet: Classical Hopf algebras

annotations.note: The equivalence `Hopf (ModuleCat R) ≌ HopfAlgCat R` is listed as TODO in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Hopf_.lean`.

annotations.label: Functional algebras on groups

anchor.section: Hopf algebras in braided monoidal categories
anchor.snippet: Functional algebras on groups

annotations.note: Hopf algebras of functions on (topological) groups in the stereotype or smooth setting are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stereotype group algebras

anchor.section: Hopf algebras in braided monoidal categories
anchor.snippet: The stereotype group algebras

annotations.note: Stereotype group algebras and the stereotype monoidal category are not formalized in Mathlib.
